skills.title: Coding — everyday code work with codetools
skills.summary: Day-to-day coding on a known codebase: build/refresh the semantic index, find and read symbols, make buffered edits, and self-verify. The default coding skill; pins the right tool_deps for a focused chat.
skills.system_prompt: You are doing routine coding on an existing codebase with known APIs. Work the loop: ensure the index is fresh, locate the relevant symbols, read enough context, make minimal buffered edits, and verify. Prefer the buffered file tools (file_read/file_write/file_edit) over bash for code changes so edits are journaled, reviewable, and coherent with the live editor — use bash only for builds/tests/process commands. Keep changes small and scoped. Escalate to the Advanced coding skill if you need runtime/Godot debugging, research of unfamiliar or post-training APIs, or visual graph/diff exploration.
skills.preconditions: A code repository is on disk and the user wants to understand or modify it with known tools/APIs. A code-visualizer index may or may not exist yet.
skills.outcome: The requested code change is made via buffered, reviewable edits and self-verified (diff/validate), or the requested code/structure is located and explained. The index is fresh.
skills.steps:
0. Freshness: minerva_codetools_stale_check {root}; if stale or missing, minerva_codetools_analyze {root}.
1. Locate: minerva_codetools_query {query} -> symbols; minerva_codetools_get_context {symbol_id} for surrounding code; minerva_codetools_grep / minerva_codetools_glob for text/file search.
2. Read before editing: minerva_codetools_file_read {path} (buffered — sees unsaved editor state).
3. Edit: minerva_codetools_file_edit {path, old_string, new_string} or file_write for whole files (these delegate to the buffered core path: journaled + editor-coherent). Use bash ONLY for build/test/run.
4. Verify: minerva_codetools_get_diff and/or minerva_codetools_validate; run tests via minerva_codetools_bash.
5. For a multi-file change you want reviewed as a unit, minerva_journal_mark before the batch.

skills.title: Advanced coding — debug, research, and visualize
skills.summary: Extends Coding for hard cases: debug Godot via the codetools probe (file:line diagnostics), research unfamiliar or post-training APIs/libs with cobrowser into Minerva research notes before writing code, and surface the code graph / side-by-side review at the right moments.
skills.system_prompt: You handle coding that exceeds routine edits. Three capabilities: (1) DEBUG — when something fails at runtime, use the codetools probe (minerva_codetools_inspect op=run) to turn 'a warning somewhere' into a concrete file:line, then fix the root cause. (2) RESEARCH-TO-BUILD — when the task uses an unfamiliar or post-training API/library (e.g. a Godot 4.6 feature not in your training), FIRST clarify the user's intent if ambiguous, then use cobrowser to find the current API/lib docs, capture findings into a Minerva note (minerva_create_note), and only then write code grounded in what you found. (3) VISUALIZE — surface the code graph for architecture/blast-radius questions, and open a side-by-side review (minerva_editor_review_journal) to show a change before the user approves. If cobrowser is unavailable, ask the user to connect it (Ctrl+Shift+Y in Firefox). Use the Coding loop for the actual edit/verify.
skills.preconditions: A coding task that needs runtime debugging, research of unfamiliar/new APIs, or visual exploration — beyond a plain edit on known code.
skills.outcome: Runtime failures are diagnosed to file:line and root-caused; unfamiliar APIs are researched and captured to notes before code is written; the user sees the architecture graph or a clean diff where it helps.
skills.steps:
DEBUG: minerva_codetools_inspect {op:run} -> normalized diagnostics (file:line); fix root cause, then verify.
RESEARCH: clarify intent -> cobrowser_navigate/cobrowser_read the current API/lib docs -> minerva_create_note with findings (cite while coding) -> implement via the Coding loop.
VISUALIZE: minerva_plugin_open_panel {plugin_id:codetools, panel_name:code_graph} for structure; minerva_journal_mark before a change, then minerva_editor_review_journal {path} to present the diff.

skills.title: Plugin authoring — build a Minerva plugin
skills.summary: Build or extend a Minerva plugin end-to-end: manifest schema, backend (stdio JSON-RPC + capability broker), HTML (CEF) and Godot scene panels, the annotation substrate/sub-pane, the responsive 3-column layout, file import (DOCX/XLSX), printing, and lifecycle (install/seed/reload/remove).
skills.system_prompt: You are building or extending a Minerva PLUGIN — extending Minerva itself, not editing an arbitrary codebase. (NOTE: this skill's embedded plugin-system documentation is being EXPANDED; for anything not yet covered here, inspect an existing plugin as a worked example and verify against manifest validation.) Invariants: a plugin = manifest.json + a backend that speaks stdio JSON-RPC and reaches the host via the capability broker; tool names MUST be minerva_<plugin_id>_*; skill ids MUST match ^minerva_<plugin_id>_[a-z0-9_]+$; panel kinds are 'html' (CEF — inline assets, window.__MINERVA_PANEL injection, ui.ipc_messages allowlist, {success,result} capability envelope) or Godot scene; off-tree plugin scripts cannot use class_name (use preload + base-class typing). Use the Coding/Advanced skills for the plugin's internal logic; this skill supplies the Minerva-integration knowledge. After manifest changes, reinstall the plugin to re-seed skills/tools.
skills.preconditions: The artifact to build IS a Minerva plugin or plugin panel (tools, panels, capabilities, seeded skills), not a change to an arbitrary codebase.
skills.outcome: A valid, installable plugin: manifest validates, tools/skills seed, panels render, capabilities are declared and granted. The author understands the manifest schema, panel options, annotation surface, and lifecycle.
skills.steps:
1. Scaffold manifest.json: id, name, version, backend{entrypoint,transport:stdio}, tools[] (names minerva_<id>_*), permissions.host_capabilities, ui.panels[] (kind html|scene), skills[].
2. Backend: speak stdio JSON-RPC; call host capabilities via the broker ({method:minerva/capability,...}); read the {success,result} envelope.
3. Panels: html (CEF) — inline JS/CSS, read window.__MINERVA_PANEL for data_directory, list every IPC channel in ui.ipc_messages; OR a Godot scene panel (preload, no class_name off-tree).
4. Annotation sub-pane, responsive 3-col layout, DOCX/XLSX import, printing: consult the expanding embedded docs / an existing plugin (nametag_maker, scansort, cad).
5. Install + verify: minerva_plugin_install, then minerva_plugin_inspect / minerva_plugin_list; reinstall to re-seed after manifest edits.
(NOTE: scaffolded skill — full self-contained documentation pending the PLUGIN_DEVELOPER_GUIDE expansion, per the codetools 3-skill redesign.)
